servinfo.title: Steering Lower Shaft Removal and Installation

attention2.ptxt: Never turn steering wheel while steering lower shaft is removed.

If it is turned and contact coil assembly gets out of centered position, the assembly must be centered again. Also, turning steering wheel more than about two and a half turns will break contact coil assembly.

s1.ptxt: Turn steering wheel so that vehicle’s front wheels are at straight-ahead position.
For keyless push start model, set ignition “LOCK”.
For ignition switch model, remove key.
Make matchmarks (1) on the following parts for correct reinstallation.

item.ptxt: Steering lower shaft (2) and shaft joint of steering column (3)
Steering lower shaft (2) and pinion shaft (4)

s1.ptxt: Remove steering lower shaft joint bolts (5) and steering column joint bolt (6).

attention2.ptxt: First insert steering lower shaft into the joint on the steering column side and then fit the joint on the other end onto pinion shaft; otherwise joints of steering lower shaft will be damaged.

s1.ptxt: Check that vehicle’s front wheels are at straight-ahead position.

Align flat part “a” on steering lower shaft (1) with bolt hole “b” of steering shaft joint (2) of steering column as shown. Then insert steering lower shaft into steering lower shaft joint of steering column while aligning matchmarks (3).
Fit joint part of steering lower shaft (1) onto pinion shaft (4) while aligning matchmarks (5).
Tighten steering lower shaft joint bolt and steering column joint bolt to specified torque in numerical order (“1” through “2”) as indicated in figure.
